natural_language: Five blocks, A through E, are arranged as follows. If you look from the table up: B is at the very bottom. Then C on top of that. Then D. Then E at the very peak. So it's a four-block column. Oh, and A is off to the side, sitting alone on the table. You can tell which ones are accessible — E (right at the top) and A (all by itself). The arm is empty and ready. The goal is to flip this column upside down. I want A at the top, then B underneath it, then C, then D, and E at the base, touching the table. So the column reverses order, and A moves from the side to become the new crown.
